properties.remark: Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from Marshall County Airport. REPORTED AT KC75.

Tornado developed 2 miles south of Henry and tracked northeast through rural portions of northern Marshall and southeast Putnam Counties, damaging trees and outbuildings. The tornado dissipated 2 miles south of McNabb. Path length 9.9 miles, max width 100 yards. Max wind speed estimated 110 mph. This updates previous LSR.

THE TORNADO WAS SIGHTED TOUCHING DOWN ABOUT A HALF MILE SW OF LATHAM, DAMAGING SOME TREES BY A HOME AND SCOURING THE GROUND IN THE FIELD AS IT HEADED TOWARD LATHAM. THE DAMAGE IN LATHAM WAS MAINLY TO TREES, WITH ONE BUSINESS BUILDING HAVING ITS ROOF BLOWN OFF. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED ON THE EAST SIDE OF LATHAM AT HIGHWAY 121. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF0 WITH PEAK WIND 85 MPH. IT WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 1.11 MILES.

NWS storm damage survey confirmed an EF-0 tornado with estimated peak winds of 85 mph near Latham, IL. The damage path length is 1.11 miles and max width of 200 yds. It was on the ground for 2 minutes and did mostly tree damage. It also blew the roof off one business building in Latham.

NWS storm survey damage survey confirmed an EF-1 tornado with estimated peak winds of 95 mph in southeast Logan and northwest Macon counties that moved southeast and then east through Warrensburg. The damage path length is 7.19 miles and max width of 300 yds. The tornado damaged several outbuildings and threw the debris to the southeast. The tornado was on the ground for 9 min and damaged several homes and numerous trees in Warrensburg and then dissipated east of town.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF 2400TH AVE AND 200TH ST SOUTHEAST OF LATHAM, DAMAGING SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND THROWING THE DEBRIS FOR 2 MILES TO THE SOUTHEAST. THE TORNADO WAS PHOTOGRAPHED WEST OF WARRENSBURG AS IT APPROACHED TOWN. THE TORNADO DAMAGED SEVERAL HOMES AND NUMEROUS TREES IN WARRENSBURG, THEN TRAVELED ALONG W. ILLINIWICK RD BEFORE DISSIPATING BEFORE REACHING N BEARSDALE RD. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF1 WITH PEAK WIND 95 MPH. IT WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 7.19 MILES.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF 2400TH AVE AND 200TH ST SOUTHEAST OF LATHAM, DAMAGING SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND THROWING THE DEBRIS FOR 2 MILES TO THE SOUTHEAST. THE TORNADO WAS PHOTOGRAPHED WEST OF WARRENSBURG AS IT APPROACHED TOWN. THE TORNADO DAMAGED SEVERAL HOMES AND NUMEROUS TREES IN WARRENSBURG, THEN TRAVELED ALONG W. ILLINIWICK RD BEFORE DISSIPATING BEFORE REACHING N BEARSDALE RD. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF1 WITH PEAK WIND 90 MPH. IT WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 7.0 MILES.

NWS storm damage survey confirmed an EF-1 tornado with estimated peak winds of 90 mph in Macon county 3 miles north-northwest of Forsyth. The damage path is 7 miles with a max width of 500 yds. The tornado was on the ground for 8 min and damaged trees, grain bins and power poles. The tornado traveled east and dissipated about 1 mile west of Argenta.

*THIS CORRECTS THE START TIME OF THE TORNADO TO 737 PM. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF 2400TH AVE AND 200TH ST SOUTHEAST OF LATHAM, DAMAGING SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND THROWING THE DEBRIS FOR 2 MILES TO THE SOUTHEAST. Rated EF2, with a peak wind speed of 130 mph. Total path length was 8.4 miles, of which 3.6 of them were in Clay County. Originally touched down in Marion County at 7:29 pm, and moved into Clay County along Beard Road just north of Songer Township Lane, downing trees and power lines. It tracked east-northeast and did its greatest damage at a farm on the north end of Penguin Drive, demolishing several metal buildings. It appears to have lifted just east of Xenia-Iola Road between Songer Township Lane and Hickory Hill Lane.
A machine shed was damaged and a few trees were blown down in Songer Township north of Xenia.
Hog barn leveled and several power poles leaning or broken. Debris from the barn was entangled in the power lines.
Numerous trees and power lines were blown down.
Several trees were blown down.
Numerous trees were blown down, including one that fell onto a camper at Charley Brown Park. No injuries reported.

Rated EF1, with a peak wind speed of 105 mph. Total path length was 6.87 miles. Touched down along E. 250th Ave. north of N. 700th St. and generally moved east- northeast. Much of the damage was limited to trees. However, a small outbuilding was destroyed near the intersection of N. 800th St. and E. 450th Ave., with a nearby home losing a small part of its roof. Some minor damage occurred near a home along N. 850th St. before the tornado lifted just north of its intersection with E. 900th Ave.

Tornado destroyed 7 grain bins and 2 power poles as it touched down 3 miles south of East Lynn. Other tree and outbuilding damage occurred over 1.3 miles before it dissipated after 2 minutes.

Path length 0.71 miles, max wind 100 mph, width 60 yards. Tracked generally eastward along E. 2600 North Rd. A farm building lost a significant part of its roof, and a home near the end of the track lost an antenna and a few windows.
Path length 0.62 miles, width 60 yards, max wind 110 mph. Generally traveled east along E. 2700 North Road, and destroyed a barn.
Semi trailer turned over on Interstate 55 just south of Chenoa.

Estimated winds 100 mph, width 80 yards, exact length to still be determined. Damage from this tornado was focused in an area between E. 2550 North and E. 2600 North Roads, and generally between N. 3100 East and N. 3300 East Roads. A farm outbuilding lost a significant portion of its roof.

Path length 0.95 miles, width 60 yards, max wind speed 110 mph. This tornado touched down just north of E. 1900 North Rd., and damaged a farmstead near the intersection with N. 4000 East Rd. A machine shed at this location was destroyed, with debris thrown a half mile east.

Path length 7.64 miles, width 1300 yards, max wind speed 110 mph. This tornado touched down a short distance from another tornado east of Anchor, but moved southeast. Several farm buildings were severely damaged or destroyed along the path. The tornado struck a farmstead at the Ford County line north of E. 1500 North Rd. A home at this location lost a significant part of its roof, and several trees were damaged. It continued southeast before lifting near the edge of Gibson City.

EF1, max wind 90 mph, path length 0.45 miles, width 50 yards. Mostly straight line wind damage occurred, with hardwood trees snapped 1/3 the way up, but mostly facing same direction. A handful of softwood trees were uprooted but positioned cyclonically. Heavy damage occurred to some but not all trailers in a mobile home park, mostly via hardwood debris and straight-line wind.

EF1, max wind 100 mph, path length 6.33 miles, max width 100 yards. This tornado touched down just east of the Illinois River, near the north edge of Billsbach Lake, and tracked southeast. Extensive tree damage occurred near Lake Wildwood.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 3 E Varna. Some shingles blown off and large branches down. Corrected time.
Tree limbs blown down onto power lines. Time estimated from radar.

EF1 strength, max wind speed 100 mph, path length 0.7 miles, max width 50 yards. Touched down in a farm field near the intersection of Hickory Grove Rd. and Washburn Rd., progressing southeast and snapping off a row of large pine trees and damaging an outbuilding.
Delayed report. Several large hardwood tree limbs down around McGraw Park area. One large tree 2 ft in diameter split, possibly due to lightning.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 NNW Central Illinois Airport. Delayed report. Several large hardwood tree limbs down around McGraw Park area. One large tree 2 ft in diameter split, possibly due to lightning.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 SSW Bloomington. Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 NNW Central Illinois Airport. Delayed report. Several large hardwood tree limbs down around Forrest Park area. One large tree 2 ft in diameter split, possibly due to lightning.

Small outbuilding destroyed. Social media report.

EF0, max wind speed 80 mph, length 4.26 miles, width 80 yards. A couple of roofs and trees were damaged in Monica before the tornado tracked east-southeast. It damaged several large trees along the path before dissipating along West Lake Shore Drive south of Princeville.

EF1, max wind speed 105 mph, path length 1 mile, max width 150 yards. This short tracked tornado touched down west of North State Route 180 and moved southeast for 1 mile doing significant tree damage along Route 180 and in the field to the east. Many of the trees were blocking the road, but were cleaned up by the time we did the survey. The tornado lifted just south of County Road 1660 North near the county line.

EF1, max wind speed 105 mph, path length 2.38 miles, max width 150 yards. This tornado touched down in a wooded area northwest of the corner of W. Legion Hall Rd. and N. Mendenhall Rd. It moved southeast where it did significant tree damage on 5 different properties, and one homestead lost 40 total trees. Many of the trees were very tall with lots of pine trees snapping, losing a third of their tops. The tornado continued to the southeast, taking down a tree onto power lines along Evans Mill Rd. It did more tree damage along Feucht Rd. and then lifted in a field east of the road.
Report from mPING: 1-inch tree limbs broken; Shingles blown off.
Estimated 65 mph wind gust with 4-6 inch tree limbs blown down and power outages.

EF0, max wind speed 85 mph, path length 2.6 miles, max width 100 yards. Touched down along W. Gun Club Rd. near the intersection with Route 91, damaging several trees. It progressed southeast and damaged homes and trees in the Trail View Estates neighborhood, before dissipating as it reached Alta.
Significant damage to siding on house on Crimson Road in Dunlap. Weather station measured wind gust 111 mph.
Measured wind gust 105 mph. Half of corn crib destroyed.

EF1, max wind speed 100 mph, path length 0.36 miles, max width 100 yards. This tornado began on the northwest side of state route 116, doing tree damage before moving southeast to the other side of the highway and causing more tree damage. The top of one pine tree was broken off and driven into the ground, about 30 yards northeast from the original tree. The tornado continued to the southeast in a heavily wooded area and dissipated.
NWS Damage Survey determined a tornado developed SE of East Peoria and progressed through Morton toward Makinaw before dissipating.

NWS Damage Survey determined a tornado developed SE of East Peoria and progressed through Morton toward Makinaw before dissipating. Tornado was on the ground for 12.39 miles for 18 minutes with peak winds of 95 mph/EF-1.
Tree blown down on Jefferson street.

5 inch trees snapped off along IL 18 east of the IL river. Downed power lines with trees blown down. Minor flooding.
Several trees were blown down, including one that fell onto a house.
Numerous large tree branches were blown down.

EF1, max wind speed 100 mph, path length 1.2 miles, max width 150 yards. This tornado started in a wooded subdivision along Woodford Way, doing tree damage to multiple trees. The tornado continued to the southeast through a newer subdivision along Tazewood Road. The tornado damaged multiple trees on one property at the end of Olivia Court and removed a small part of the roof and some siding, creating a hole in the roof. This house also had one window blown in on the back side of the house. The tornado continued southeast, doing more tree damage and at the southeast corner of Tazewood Road and Nofsinger Road. At this corner, the tornado damaged multiple old trees on the property, including uprooting two of the trees. The tornado dissipated at this location.
Around 50 large trees including oak trees blown down at Springdale Cemetery in Peoria.
Large tree snapped in several places.
Four to 6 inch tree limbs blown down with power hits on north Grand Blvd.

Corrects previous tornado report from 2 NW West Jersey. Delayed Report: With support from drone footage, a trained spotter reported the tornado touched down near Walnut Creek 3 miles NW of West Jersey moving mainly through crop land before causing tree damage west of State Route 78.
